key_insight: A transfer-learning update to BiSeNetV2 with a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss makes seam segmentation much more robust to reflective welding surfaces without increasing model complexity.
engineering_relevance: The method improves real-time robotic welding perception in construction, where thin seams and specular glare can cause critical segmentation failures.
method: Start from a BiSeNetV2 backbone and apply transfer learning for seam segmentation.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to improve boundary quality and overlap stability.
Keep the architecture lightweight so FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed remain unchanged.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline and recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failures.
watch_item: The gains appear strongest under reflective conditions, so deployment should still be validated on diverse weld geometries, lighting setups, and camera positions.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
